procedure_name: Consolidation Chemotherapy for Acute Myeloid Leukemia
source_files: PMJAY_STG_Acute Myeloid Leukemia & Acute Promyelocytic Leukemia.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: DM (Medical oncology) or equivalent (MD Medicine with experience/Fellowship in Hematology/Medical Oncology)
clinical_indications: Complete remission after initial induction Chemotherapy with good risk
Complete remission after initial induction chemotherapy with intermediate/ poor risk where the patient is unwilling/ no match for bone marrow transplant

preauth.label: Bone marrow report confirming complete remission after induction (typically done on day 21-28 after induction chemotherapy)
Prior treatment with chemotherapy (if second time): regimen, dates, response (if applicable)

claim.label: Reports of tests supporting diagnosis (pathology, radiology, microbiology, hematology, biochemistry, etc.)
Discharge summary of day care department
Periodic CBC reports (before start of consolidation and then once a week later till all consolidation is completed)
All documents as mentioned in preauthorization

ppd_preauth.q: Poor performance status (3/4) for AML consolidation?
Bone marrow report confirming complete remission after induction available?

cpd_claim.q: Periodic CBC reports available before and during consolidation?
Same package re-claimed within 6 months for AML consolidation?

common_queries: Bone marrow report not confirming complete remission after induction for AML consolidation.
Periodic CBC reports missing for the consolidation period.
Barcode of administered drugs or diagnosis supporting reports missing at claim stage.
Package re-claimed within 6 months without justification for AML consolidation.
Clinical notes indicate poor risk for bone marrow transplant without documented patient unwillingness or no match.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Qualified Medical Oncologist/hematologist; Chemotherapy unit (Day care and Ward)
clinical_key_pointers: Rest all packages can be repeated in second line therapy if last treatment was >6 months back and patient achieved complete response to first line therapy
contraindications: Age > 65 years with multiple co-morbidities, poor general condition, high risk disease (AML only)
